Map guide

Everything you need to get the most out of this map — whether you're browsing here or using the Google Maps version on your phone.

🌿 About this map

A growing collection of over 100 gardens across the Netherlands, chosen for their beauty, variety, and photography potential. New gardens, descriptions, and photos are added over time.

Featured gardens include detailed descriptions, sensory notes, and photography tips written from personal visits. If you visit a garden, tag me on Instagram — @marianne_eggink — I'd love to see what you find. 🌿

👆 How to browse
  • ✦
    Tap any card to read the full description and find visitor links.
  • ✦
    Search by name, filter by category, or narrow by province using the dropdown.
  • ✦
    Cards with a 🎥 Instagram Reel button have been featured in the Wander & Bloom series — tap to watch the walk.
  • ✦
    Cards with a 📍 Google Maps button link directly to the garden's location.
🗺️ Using the Google Maps version
Open in Google Maps

The Google Maps version shows all gardens on a full interactive map — useful for planning a route or combining with your own navigation.

  • 1
    Open via the button above, or through the link in the Instagram bio.
  • 2
    The map will open in your browser or in the Google Maps app. Once opened in the app, it is automatically saved to your Google Maps app under Your Places → Maps → Saved. Under Maps you can toggle the Wander & Bloom map on and off.
  • 3
    Tap any marker to see the garden name and description.
  • 4
    Tap Directions to navigate there as normal.
💡 Best on desktop or Android — links in garden descriptions are fully clickable there.
📱 iPhone users: There is a long-standing compatibility issue in Google Maps on iOS where links inside descriptions are not clickable. That is part of why this website exists. Use the Browse List and Full Map tabs here instead. If you do want to use the Google Maps version, use the name of the garden to search it.

About Wander & Bloom

A free photography garden map of the Netherlands

Marianne Eggink
Journalist · Editor · Mindful photographer · Amsterdam
I picked up my first camera at eight years old — my father's analogue one — and I have been noticing the world through a lens ever since. My earliest subjects were the grapes and roses in my parents' garden. Photography is how I connect with nature, and with myself: a practice of honest presence, of slowing down enough to actually see.

As an autistic person, photography has also been a tool for grounding — for noticing what is actually here, and slowly befriending yourself in the process. Wander & Bloom grew out of that same impulse: to pause, to wander, and to notice what blooms along the way.

What started as a personal list of gardens I wanted to visit grew into something bigger — over 100 gardens across the Netherlands, added by hand. Some I have visited myself and documented with photography reels, sensory notes, and tips for what to look for with a camera. Others are still on my bucket list to visit later. Every entry has been chosen with care, for anyone who moves through the world at their own pace and finds meaning in green spaces.

If you visit a garden you found here, I would love to see what you notice — tag me on Instagram.

Wander & Bloom — Garden map of the Netherlands
